# A Vindication of the Rights of Woman

* Mary Wollstonecraft (author)

Wollstonecraft first defended the rights of men in response to Burke’s pamphlet on the French Revolution, then turned to the rights of woman a couple of years later. It is one of the key texts of modern feminist thought.

Read Now

#### Citation

_A Vindication of the Rights of Woman with Strictures on Political and Moral Subjects_ (London: J. Johnson, 1792).

#### Copyright

The text is in the public domain.

## Related People

Mary Wollstonecraft Shelley (Author) English novelist, author of Frankenstein and Mathilda, Mary Wollstonecraft Shelley was the daughter of Mary Wollstonecraft and wife of the poet Percy Bysshe Shelley.

## Connected Readings

Book

A Vindication of the Rights of Men

Mary Wollstonecraft

a Letter to the Right Honourable Edmund Burke

One of the first responses to Burke’s Reflections on the Revolution in France. Wollstonecraft later lived in France during the Terror and wrote a history of the events she witnessed.

Book

An Historical and Moral View of the Origin and Progress of the French Revolution

Mary Wollstonecraft

An optimistic and rationalist account of the French Revolution based upon first-hand, eyewitness experiences. It was intended to defend the Revolution against critics like Burke.
